summary: Cleaned up Stamina mode game-mode code by replacing anonymous temporaries and struct fields with meaningful names, documenting elimination/endgame counters, using known SSSData and StartMeleeData types, and hiding an internal GObj proc callback from the public gmstamina header.
what_changed: Renamed fields in the static gm_804975F8 state struct from x0, x4, and x14 to done_counter, eliminated, and slomo_counter.

Added comments explaining that done_counter ticks up to 100 when the match is done, eliminated tracks whether each slot has been eliminated, and slomo_counter ticks up to 100 after player elimination.
Replaced local temporary names such as temp_r31, temp_r30, and temp_r29 with semantic variables vs, sss, and start.
Replaced an inline anonymous SSS-like struct in gm_801B927C with SSSData returned from gm_801A4284.
Updated SSS access from raw fields x3 and x4 to force_stage_id and start_game.
Kept Stamina setup behavior intact while expressing it through StartMeleeData and VsModeData names: setting rules, players, one stock, and 150 HP.
Added documentation comments for gm_801B9600 as the number of players or teams remaining and for fn_801B96E8 as handling player elimination and game end.
Made fn_801B96E8 static in gmstamina.c and removed its declaration plus an unnecessary HSD forward include from gmstamina.h.
Changed magic constant 0x64 to decimal 100 in the elimination slowdown and match-end counters.
smart_moves: The PR improved readability without broad behavioral churn: the diff is mostly renames, type substitution, comments, and header pruning.
Using SSSData instead of an anonymous guessed struct converted uncertain field access into known menu/start-game semantics.
Renaming gm_804975F8 fields made the stamina match loop self-explanatory: eliminated slots, per-player slowdown countdowns, and delayed match termination are now visible from the code.
Making fn_801B96E8 static correctly reflects that it is only used as an internal GObj process callback created by fn_801B9850.
Removing the HSD_GObj forward include from the header followed naturally from no longer exporting the callback signature.
decomp_lessons: When a temporary anonymous struct is suspected to be a known scene payload, replacing it with the project type such as SSSData can both document intent and reduce duplicate layout guesses.

Static file-local state structs should get semantic field names as soon as behavior is understood; counters and boolean arrays are especially valuable to name because they clarify control-flow conditions.
Do not expose internal process callbacks in headers unless another translation unit needs them; making callbacks static can simplify includes and reduce false API surface.
Small comments on timer thresholds are useful in game-mode code where values like 100 frames/ticks gate transitions and slowdown recovery.
Renaming compiler-style temporaries to domain names such as vs, sss, and start makes scene transition code easier to audit for matching and logic preservation.
naming_conventions: Use done_counter for a global countdown/tick counter that delays ending the match after one player/team remains.
Use eliminated[slot] for per-player slot elimination state instead of anonymous bool arrays.
Use slomo_counter[slot] for per-player counters controlling temporary slow-motion after elimination.
Use concise domain local names like vs for VsModeData, sss for SSSData, and start for StartMeleeData.
Known SSSData fields start_game and force_stage_id are preferred over raw x4 and x3 field names when handling stage-select results.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: The cleanup preserved matching-sensitive structure while renaming fields and locals, indicating these readability improvements did not require algorithmic changes.
Retained PAD_STACK(4) in fn_801B96E8, suggesting the stack padding remained necessary for matching.
Changed fn_801B96E8 to static while keeping its function body and GObjProc registration usage intact.
Replaced 0x64 with 100 for the same threshold, making the frame/tick limit clearer without changing behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were included in the provided PR slice.
The PR had one issue comment according to counts, but no comment body was present in the provided context, so no substantive reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Find other game-mode files that expose internal GObj process callbacks in headers and can be made static.
Search for anonymous structs returned from gm_801A4284 or gm_801A427C that should be replaced with SSSData or StartMeleeData.
Audit remaining gmstamina symbols for unmapped function names and remaining x-prefixed rule/player fields.
Look for other static state structs with x0/x4/x14 style fields where behavior is now understood well enough to name counters or flags.
